title: Requirements, ER Modeling, Functional Dependencies, and Normalization · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A growing incident system repeats team and member facts, mixes multiple values per cell, and produces update anomalies. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a normalized incident-system schema defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive entities, attributes, identifiers, relationships, optionality, and business rules from stakeholder language.
Model one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with minimum and maximum cardinality.
Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;
